raw_consumer_statement:
I am filing this complaint because Barclays Bank has failed to conduct a reasonable investigation into a fraudulent account opened in my name. 

On XX/XX/XXXX, a fraudulent purchase of {$310.00} was made on a Barclays Gap Good Rewards Mastercard  opened under my identity. On the same exact date, XX/XX/XXXX, a separate fraudulent XXXX XXXX credit card account was also opened and used under my identity. 

Upon discovering the fraud, I immediately contacted both issuers, froze my credit with all three credit bureaus, filed an Identity Theft Report with the Federal Trade Commission ( IdentityTheft.gov reference # XXXX ), and filed a complaint with the XXXX XXXX XXXX  XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX ( reference # XXXX ). 

XXXX XXXX conducted an investigation and formally determined that I was not responsible for the fraudulent account. They notified the credit reporting agencies and the account was removed from my credit report ( letter dated XX/XX/XXXX ). 

Both the XXXX XXXX account and the Barclays Gap Good Rewards Mastercard were opened and used on the same exact date as part of the same identity theft incident. Despite this, Barclays continues to assert that the Gap Good Rewards Mastercard account is valid without explaining how the account was verified as belonging to me. 

Additionally, XXXX  has already removed this account from my credit report, yet the account continues to appear on XXXX, demonstrating inconsistent reporting of the same account across credit bureaus. 

Because I submitted an Identity Theft Report under FCRA 605B and provided documentation of identity theft, Barclays continued reporting of this account without providing evidence of verification raises concerns that the investigation may not have met the requirements of the Fair Credit Reporting Act.

Barclays has repeatedly asserted that the account is valid but has never provided any documentation showing how the account was verified as belonging to me ( such as the application record, identity verification method, or device/IP information used to open the account ). 

I am requesting that Barclays provide documentation explaining how this account was verified as belonging to me and correct the inaccurate reporting associated with this fraudulent account.
